testTickets.question: Does COMPLIANCE build, install, appear in the expected host/plugin browser, instantiate, validate, and replace older builds cleanly?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if the current build installs cleanly, appears under THETA Audio Research where applicable, and opens without scan or instantiation failure.
testTickets.testMaterial: Ableton/Logic/Standalone as applicable

testTickets.question: Does COMPLIANCE pass and process audio without unexpected silence, crackle, hum, unsafe level jumps, or host instability?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail immediately for silence, runaway level, persistent crackle, stuck output, or repeatable host crash.
testTickets.testMaterial: default preset, INIT/START preset, heavy preset, bypass toggles

testTickets.question: Does COMPLIANCE sound like a broadcast/dialnorm-aware limiter that controls loudness transparently without flattening dialogue or transient life?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if the sonic identity remains clear in level-matched bypass comparisons and on at least three source types.
testTickets.testMaterial: drums, bass, vocal/dialogue, synth/drone, full mix as relevant

testTickets.question: Across subtle, medium, and extreme settings, does COMPLIANCE remain musically useful instead of only working in one narrow range?
testTickets.acceptance: Notes must identify best setting range, failure range, and at least one ideal use case.
testTickets.testMaterial: subtle setting, medium setting, pushed/extreme setting

testTickets.question: Do limiter modes, oversampling, ceiling/input behavior, multichannel handling, LFE behavior, meters, and loudness target coloring behave correctly?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if each major module/control has audible or visible cause-and-effect and no stale UI/audio state.
testTickets.testMaterial: module-by-module sweep

testTickets.question: Do COMPLIANCE module toggles, modes, macros, bypass states, and extreme settings avoid stuck states, clicks, NaN, runaway feedback, or inconsistent behavior?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail if any control path can break audio, desync UI, or require plugin reload to recover.
testTickets.testMaterial: extreme parameter sweep, rapid toggle/mode changes

testTickets.question: Can COMPLIANCE be read, clicked, resized, and understood without fighting the interface?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if labels are readable, hit areas feel correct, active states are obvious, and the flow is understandable within 30 seconds.
testTickets.testMaterial: default scale, common laptop display, resize if supported

testTickets.question: Does COMPLIANCE show the expected version/build identity and no stale legacy product naming in user-facing UI or installed artifacts?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail if version is wrong/missing or old product identity leaks into visible labels, package names, or host display.
testTickets.testMaterial: UI footer/header, plugin browser, installed bundles

testTickets.question: Do COMPLIANCE presets and DAW session reload preserve audio state, UI state, selected modes, and bypass states correctly?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if save/reopen restores both sound and visible state.
testTickets.testMaterial: factory presets, user preset if available, DAW save/reopen

testTickets.question: Does COMPLIANCE automation and offline render match realtime playback without clicks, zippering, or missing processing?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail if common automated parameters click badly or offline bounce differs obviously from realtime.
testTickets.testMaterial: automation lanes, realtime playback, offline bounce

testTickets.question: Does COMPLIANCE stay responsive with acceptable CPU, stable meters/visuals, and no denormal spikes during silence or heavy use?
testTickets.acceptance: Notes should identify default CPU, heavy CPU, silence behavior, and any UI redraw sluggishness.
testTickets.testMaterial: host CPU meter, silence, heavy preset, moving UI/meters

testTickets.question: Compared with the previous known-good build, did COMPLIANCE keep the important behavior while avoiding new regressions?
testTickets.acceptance: Notes must identify what improved, what regressed, and whether the build is safer than the previous baseline.
testTickets.testMaterial: previous known-good build notes, current build

testTickets.question: What reference audio, screenshots, tester notes, or external examples should be brought back into the COMPLIANCE project chat?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass when there is enough evidence to guide the next build without relying on memory.
testTickets.testMaterial: screenshots, audio examples, tester notes, return packet

testTickets.question: Based on testing, what is the exact next build direction for COMPLIANCE?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only when the next action is clear: bugfix, DSP pass, UI pass, installer pass, or ship/hold.
testTickets.testMaterial: completed smoke/deep QA tickets

ticketCategoryDescriptions.INSTALL / HOST: Does it build, install, appear, instantiate, validate, and replace old versions?
ticketCategoryDescriptions.BASIC AUDIO / STABILITY: Does audio pass, avoid silence/crackle/hum/level jumps, and survive normal use?
ticketCategoryDescriptions.SONIC CHARACTER: Does the product sound like itself? Does it improve material in a level-matched test?
ticketCategoryDescriptions.MODULE BEHAVIOR: Do individual modules, modes, macros, and edge cases behave as designed?
ticketCategoryDescriptions.INTERFACE / UX: Can you see, read, click, understand, resize, and operate it without fighting the UI?
ticketCategoryDescriptions.PRESETS / STATE / AUTOMATION: Do presets, session recall, bypass, automation, offline render, and state restoration work?
ticketCategoryDescriptions.PERFORMANCE / REGRESSION: CPU, spikes, denormals, meters, graph redraws, oversampling, stability under stress.
ticketCategoryDescriptions.ROADMAP / EVIDENCE: Reference recordings, tester feedback, hardware comparison notes, and return-to-chat evidence.

projects.qaStrategy: Smoke tickets are ship gates; Deep QA tickets are grouped by category and should drive return packets.

testTickets.question: Does CREOSOTE still sound like a high-end analog character processor, not a generic saturation/EQ/compressor stack?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if the plugin has a clear sonic identity at subtle, medium, and pushed settings.
testTickets.testMaterial: drums, bass, vocal, synth/drone, full mix

testTickets.question: On level-matched bypass, does CREOSOTE improve weight, depth, motion, glue, or tone without merely getting louder?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if the improvement remains clear after output level is matched to bypass.
testTickets.testMaterial: drums, bass, vocal, full mix

testTickets.question: Which real sources benefit from CREOSOTE, and which expose problems?
testTickets.acceptance: Notes must mention at least three source types and identify best/worst use cases.
testTickets.testMaterial: drums, bass, vocal, synth/drone, full mix

testTickets.question: Does CREOSOTE add useful low-mid density and body without muddying 150–500 Hz?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if added weight does not blur bass/kick/vocal fundamentals.
testTickets.testMaterial: bass, drums, full mix

testTickets.question: Does the top end stay expensive and controlled, or does it become fizzy, dull, harsh, or phasey?
testTickets.acceptance: Notes should identify whether the top end feels polished, dark, brittle, smeared, or unstable.
testTickets.testMaterial: vocal, cymbals, full mix

testTickets.question: As input, drive, or intensity increases, does CREOSOTE progress musically from subtle to rich to aggressive?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail if the usable range is tiny, jumpy, or only works at one setting.
testTickets.testMaterial: drums, bass, full mix

testTickets.question: Does GRIT deliver believable tape/hysteresis movement across subtle, medium, and pushed settings?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if GRIT has a distinct use case from AMBER and CHARACTER.
testTickets.testMaterial: drums, bass, full mix

testTickets.question: Do AMBER era/tube voices feel meaningfully different, useful, and not just EQ variations?
testTickets.acceptance: Notes must describe the audible difference between voices.
testTickets.testMaterial: vocal, bass, synth, full mix

testTickets.question: Do tube condition/age controls add character without collapsing into noise, dullness, or gimmick?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if aging/condition settings remain musically usable over a reasonable range.
testTickets.testMaterial: vocal, synth, full mix

testTickets.question: Do the COMPRESS modes each have a clear musical use case with no obvious pumping, clicking, or level instability?
testTickets.acceptance: Notes must identify which mode is most useful and which needs work.
testTickets.testMaterial: drums, bass, full mix

testTickets.question: Do SUMMING width, crosstalk, balance, pregain, and headroom feel coherent and mix-safe?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail if stereo image collapses, phase gets weird, or balance shifts unexpectedly.
testTickets.testMaterial: stereo drums, stereo synth, full mix

testTickets.question: Does CHARACTER feel like a useful master identity control rather than a duplicate of GRIT/AMBER?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if CHARACTER changes the plugin identity in an intentional and controllable way.
testTickets.testMaterial: drums, vocal, full mix

testTickets.question: Do OUT CLIP and LIMITER work together musically, catching peaks without dulling transient life?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail if clipping/limiting causes brittle distortion, pumping, or unexpected level jumps.
testTickets.testMaterial: drums, full mix

testTickets.question: With bands enabled, does EQ master OFF fully bypass EQ processing?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if all audible EQ processing stops when EQ master is OFF.
testTickets.testMaterial: pink noise, full mix, vocal

testTickets.question: Does the static/live EQ graph match what you hear, especially with dynamic bands active?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail if graph movement implies processing that is not audible, or misses processing that is audible.
testTickets.testMaterial: pink noise, vocal, drums

testTickets.question: Does positive DYN create useful upward movement and negative DYN create useful suppression?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if up/down dynamic behavior is obvious enough to trust.
testTickets.testMaterial: vocal, drums, bass

testTickets.question: Do HP and LP filters audibly remove low/high content enough to trust the graph?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail if filters are visually active but sonically too weak or inconsistent.
testTickets.testMaterial: full mix, pink noise

testTickets.question: Can a user understand the signal flow and major sections within 30 seconds?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if signal flow, sections, and active states are visually obvious.
testTickets.testMaterial: UI inspection

testTickets.question: Are knobs, tabs, buttons, and graph nodes easy to click without accidental adjacent actions?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail if any frequent control feels fiddly or error-prone.
testTickets.testMaterial: UI inspection

testTickets.question: Do ON/OFF states, dimming, fills, meters, and active indicators make the processing state obvious?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if bypassed/inactive modules are visually unambiguous.
testTickets.testMaterial: UI inspection

testTickets.question: Are there no visible EROSION-era labels, filenames, or identity leaks in user-facing UI?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail if legacy product identity appears anywhere a tester/user can see it.

testTickets.question: Does 'ENABLE_AAX=1 ./make_installer.sh' run clean end to end — AAX PACE-signs and verifies, the pkg builds, and Apple's notary returns success (stapled) for BOTH the pkg and the DMG?
testTickets.notes: Watch point: the AAX surviving notarisation. If notary rejects it, that is the first ticket — grab the log, do not hand-fix.

testTickets.question: Installing the notarised DMG and launching the host, is there no Gatekeeper/quarantine block or warn ('unidentified developer', 'damaged', quarantine)?
testTickets.notes: Clean rig. Record the EXACT dialog wording if anything appears. Expected: none.

testTickets.question: Does a plain ad-hoc (codesign --sign -) build get blocked on the same clean rig, proving notarisation is the fix?
testTickets.notes: Optional A/B. Items 0-2 are the beta gate.

testTickets.question: Is CATHODE.vst3 in /Library/Audio/Plug-Ins/VST3 and seen by the host (no iLok needed)?
Is CATHODE.component in /Library/Audio/Plug-Ins/Components and does it pass the host's first-launch AU scan (no iLok needed)?
Does CATHODE.aaxplugin land in /Library/Application Support/Avid/Audio/Plug-Ins (install + presence only here)?
testTickets.notes: Actual loading is in the AAX / PRO TOOLS section, since it needs your iLok present.

testTickets.question: Does CATHODE load and instantiate in Ableton Live, with the window opening?
Does CATHODE pass AU validation in Logic and instantiate?
Does CATHODE load in one more host (Reaper / Bitwig / Studio One), confirming it is not host-specific?
Does the window open at 1280x640, render fully, and show 'v16.97'?
Does adding/removing the plugin and opening/closing the UI ~10x stay stable (no crash, no creep)?
Does a saved project with CATHODE + settings reopen with state intact?
Do host bypass and plugin BYPASS both null cleanly with no level jump?
On drums, does ATTACK punch the onset above noon and soften it below noon, with the meter spiking to match?
Does SUSTAIN shape the body just after the hit in the correct direction?
Does DECAY (new) shape the tail (up = longer/fuller, down = tighter) and read clearly distinct from SUSTAIN?
With heavy CRUSH/WARBLE running, does flipping LFO INPUT to CHAIN END move where the shaping lands, with no click on the switch?
With TONE depth armed on a full-band source, is the dark-to-bright wobble clearly stronger (now +/-15 dB) and still usable at full depth?
Do VOL / PAN / WIDTH / TONE / WARBLE each do nothing at depth 0 and engage as depth comes up?
Do all 8 factory LFO presets load and sound sane, with transient params neutral and routing = LFO input on load?
Does AUTO LEVEL on/off change the output-ceiling behaviour with no instability?
Do LT_TRANS_DEC, LT_TRANS_POS and a couple of other params automate cleanly (track, no zipper, no crash)?
At 44.1 / 48 / 96 kHz, do the meter and DSP behave?
At 64 and 1024 sample buffers, is there no glitch or crackle?
Is silence-in silence-out, and does hammering params during playback produce no NaN blow-up?
Over a few minutes with LFO + transient active, is there no drift and no crash?
Does Pro Tools scan and validate CATHODE on launch, appearing in the plugin list with no 'failed to load' or blacklist?
testTickets.notes: Run on the rig where your iLok / PACE auth lives.

testTickets.question: Does the AAX load without an auth/licence error (wraptool verify --verbose --in <installed .aaxplugin> passes)?
testTickets.notes: AAX is PACE-signed to your account; it needs that authorisation present to load.

testTickets.question: Inserting CATHODE on a PT track, does the UI open at full size and audio pass?
In PT, do the transient designer (ATTACK/SUSTAIN/DECAY), live meter, routing toggle, and LFO destinations all behave as in the other formats?
Writing and reading automation on a couple of PT params, is there no glitch?
Does saving and reopening a PT session with CATHODE keep state intact?
Does inserting/removing/rescanning a few times in PT produce no PT-specific crash?
Does the button read TRANS (not EDIT), click into the transient page, and BACK return to main?
Does the live gain meter scroll with 'now' at the right edge, lift on boost and drop on cut (+/-12 dB scale), and show live gain in dB + routing text in the title row?
Are the six main rows in order (SHAPE/SYNC, VOL/PAN, WIDTH/TONE, WARBLE/RATE, PHASE/SMOOTH, OUTPUT WIDTH full-width) with no overlap or clipping at the 1280 minimum width?
Is the DECAY pill present, labelled, and sitting cleanly beside the routing toggle?
Does the routing toggle read LFO INPUT / CHAIN END, flip on click, and follow a preset that sets it?
Is LOSS now olive (matching DULL and the other sliders), with no other stray gold or odd-coloured pills anywhere?
testTickets.notes: Sweep the whole UI for stray colours.

testTickets.question: Does the LFO scope still show on both pages and track the shape?
Bouncing main to TRANS to scope repeatedly, does nothing get stuck and the meter resume cleanly?
Does every slider/knob/dropdown on every module move and read correctly, with correct labels?
Does 'v16.97' appear everywhere the version is shown?
Do the logo and THETA / AUDIO RESEARCH render correctly?
With ATTACK/SUSTAIN now reading off the fast/mid envelope pair (changed in v16.95), does the onset response feel right, or want re-tuning before beta?
testTickets.notes: Flag now if it wants re-tuning.

testTickets.question: Any visual glitch, mislabel, or rough edge you would not want a tester to see?

testTickets.question: Does HIERARCHY make mix priority and ducking decisions feel intentional, transparent, and controllable?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if the sonic identity remains clear in level-matched bypass comparisons and on at least three source types.
testTickets.testMaterial: drums, bass, vocal/dialogue, synth/drone, full mix as relevant

testTickets.question: Across subtle, medium, and extreme settings, does HIERARCHY remain musically useful instead of only working in one narrow range?
testTickets.acceptance: Notes must identify best setting range, failure range, and at least one ideal use case.
testTickets.testMaterial: subtle setting, medium setting, pushed/extreme setting

testTickets.question: Do routing, shared-memory/IPC if present, priority lanes, band ducking, host visibility, and session behavior work correctly?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if each major module/control has audible or visible cause-and-effect and no stale UI/audio state.
testTickets.testMaterial: module-by-module sweep

testTickets.question: Do HIERARCHY module toggles, modes, macros, bypass states, and extreme settings avoid stuck states, clicks, NaN, runaway feedback, or inconsistent behavior?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail if any control path can break audio, desync UI, or require plugin reload to recover.
testTickets.testMaterial: extreme parameter sweep, rapid toggle/mode changes

testTickets.question: Can HIERARCHY be read, clicked, resized, and understood without fighting the interface?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if labels are readable, hit areas feel correct, active states are obvious, and the flow is understandable within 30 seconds.
testTickets.testMaterial: default scale, common laptop display, resize if supported

testTickets.question: Does HIERARCHY show the expected version/build identity and no stale legacy product naming in user-facing UI or installed artifacts?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail if version is wrong/missing or old product identity leaks into visible labels, package names, or host display.
testTickets.testMaterial: UI footer/header, plugin browser, installed bundles

testTickets.question: Do HIERARCHY presets and DAW session reload preserve audio state, UI state, selected modes, and bypass states correctly?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only if save/reopen restores both sound and visible state.
testTickets.testMaterial: factory presets, user preset if available, DAW save/reopen

testTickets.question: Does HIERARCHY automation and offline render match realtime playback without clicks, zippering, or missing processing?
testTickets.acceptance: Fail if common automated parameters click badly or offline bounce differs obviously from realtime.
testTickets.testMaterial: automation lanes, realtime playback, offline bounce

testTickets.question: Does HIERARCHY stay responsive with acceptable CPU, stable meters/visuals, and no denormal spikes during silence or heavy use?
testTickets.acceptance: Notes should identify default CPU, heavy CPU, silence behavior, and any UI redraw sluggishness.
testTickets.testMaterial: host CPU meter, silence, heavy preset, moving UI/meters

testTickets.question: Compared with the previous known-good build, did HIERARCHY keep the important behavior while avoiding new regressions?
testTickets.acceptance: Notes must identify what improved, what regressed, and whether the build is safer than the previous baseline.
testTickets.testMaterial: previous known-good build notes, current build

testTickets.question: What reference audio, screenshots, tester notes, or external examples should be brought back into the HIERARCHY project chat?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass when there is enough evidence to guide the next build without relying on memory.
testTickets.testMaterial: screenshots, audio examples, tester notes, return packet

testTickets.question: Based on testing, what is the exact next build direction for HIERARCHY?
testTickets.acceptance: Pass only when the next action is clear: bugfix, DSP pass, UI pass, installer pass, or ship/hold.
testTickets.testMaterial: completed smoke/deep QA tickets

ticketCategoryDescriptions.INSTALL / HOST: Does it build, install, appear, instantiate, validate, and replace old versions?
ticketCategoryDescriptions.BASIC AUDIO / STABILITY: Does audio pass, avoid silence/crackle/hum/level jumps, and survive normal use?
ticketCategoryDescriptions.SONIC CHARACTER: Does the product sound like itself? Does it improve material in a level-matched test?
ticketCategoryDescriptions.MODULE BEHAVIOR: Do individual modules, modes, macros, and edge cases behave as designed?
ticketCategoryDescriptions.INTERFACE / UX: Can you see, read, click, understand, resize, and operate it without fighting the UI?
ticketCategoryDescriptions.PRESETS / STATE / AUTOMATION: Do presets, session recall, bypass, automation, offline render, and state restoration work?
ticketCategoryDescriptions.PERFORMANCE / REGRESSION: CPU, spikes, denormals, meters, graph redraws, oversampling, stability under stress.
ticketCategoryDescriptions.ROADMAP / EVIDENCE: Reference recordings, tester feedback, hardware comparison notes, and return-to-chat evidence.

projects.qaStrategy: Smoke tickets are ship gates; Deep QA tickets are grouped by category and should drive return packets.
